message.content: Adversaries may exploit Domain Policy Modification: Domain Trust Modification to add or alter domain trusts, thereby evading defenses and elevating privileges. By manipulating domain trust properties, attackers can access shared resources across domains without needing to compromise signing certificates. This technique allows adversaries to forge SAML tokens or convert domains to federated domains, enabling unauthorized access and privilege escalation. Tools like AADInternals can facilitate these modifications, creating backdoors and altering authentication settings. For instance, APT29 has used Azure AD administrative permissions to change domain federation trust settings, allowing them to use their own SAML signing certificates for unauthorized access. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and auditing domain trust changes, implementing strict access controls, and using multi-factor authentication to reduce the risk of unauthorized modifications.
